    "text": "function armFundamentalPage() {\n  const c = DATA.companies['ARM'];\n  const fs_peers = (globalThis.ARM_FACTSET_PEERS) || {};\n  const target = (fs_peers && fs_peers.target) || { price: 212.65, mkt_cap_B: 242.43 };\n  const spot = (c && c.technical && c.technical.spot_price) || target.price || 212.65;\n  const toc = [\n    { id:'arm-fa-overview', label:'Overview' },\n    { id:'arm-fa-keyratios', label:'Key ratios' },\n    { id:'arm-fa-qvq', label:'Quarter-vs-quarter' },\n    { id:'arm-fa-trajectory', label:'Revenue growth trajectory' },\n    { id:'arm-fa-charts', label:'Charts' },\n    { id:'arm-fa-interp', label:'Institutional interpretation' },\n  ];\n  return `\n    ${typeof reportHero === 'function' ? reportHero({\n      kicker:'FUNDAMENTAL — ARM Holdings',\n      title:'ARM — Quarter-to-Quarter Comparative Analysis',\n      subtitle:'Royalty + license model. FY26 outlook anchored by AI-driven Compute Subsystems and v9 royalty mix.',\n      barColor:'var(--accent)',\n      badges: [\n        { label:'Latest verdict', value: c?.q2q_analysis?.q_to_q_pairs?.slice(-1)?.[0]?.verdict || '—', color:'var(--accent)', sub:'Most recent reported quarter' },\n        { label:'MCS (info-adj)', value: ((c?.mcs_information_adjusted || 0) * 100).toFixed(1) + '%', color:'var(--mint)', sub:'8-claim accuracy' },\n        { label:'Rows tracked', value: String((c?.rows || []).length), color:'var(--mint)', sub:'Forward-looking claims' },\n        { label:'Pairs', value: String((c?.q2q_analysis?.q_to_q_pairs || []).length), color:'var(--mint)', sub:'Said → Actual' },\n      ],\n      dataSource:'SEC 20-F/6-K filings · ARM Holdings transcripts · ranking_engine/inputs'\n    }) : ''}\n    ${typeof reportTOC === 'function' ? reportTOC(toc) : ''}\n\n    ${typeof reportSection === 'function' ? reportSection('Overview', `\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;\">ARM Holdings is the world's most-licensed CPU IP company. Revenue splits between upfront <strong>licenses</strong> (lumpy, large contracts) and recurring <strong>royalties</strong> (per-chip, growth tracks v9 adoption + AI/HPC). Margin profile is software-like — ~95% gross margins — with the operating-margin lever largely a function of R&amp;D pacing.</p>\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;margin-top:8px;\">Recent quarters show: (i) Compute Subsystems traction with hyperscalers (Microsoft, Meta, NVIDIA, AWS) driving forward royalty mix toward higher-rate v9 + ARMv9 designs; (ii) Smartphone royalty steady-to-up despite mature TAM; (iii) Automotive + IoT verticals exhibiting double-digit unit growth.</p>\n    `, { id:'arm-fa-overview' }) : ''}\n\n    ${typeof reportSection === 'function' ? reportSection('Key ratios — TTM and latest quarter', `\n      <p style=\"font-size:11.5px;color:var(--muted);font-style:italic;margin:0 0 14px 0;\">All ratios computed from ARM 20-F (FY2025) and 6-K quarterly press releases. style=\"font-size:12.5px;color:var(--muted);margin-top:14px;line-height:1.55;font-style:italic;\">FY26-Q4 was a continuation of the AI-infrastructure cycle peak, with quarter-over-quarter modest growth in revenue and EPS as the seasonal mobile-OEM ramp drove royalty units lower than the AGI CPU-heavy FY26-Q3. Operating margin held above 43%, validating the structural inflection.</p>\n    `, { id:'arm-fa-qvq' }) : ''}\n\n    ${typeof reportSection === 'function' ? reportSection('Revenue growth trajectory — through the AI infrastructure cycle', `\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;\">ARM revenue compounded from $939M (FY25-Q1) to $1.49B (FY26-Q4) — a <strong>+59% cumulative lift over 7 quarters</strong>. The 8-quarter TTM revenue of $${((5150.0/1000).toFixed(2))}B is up +28.6% YoY (vs $${((4006.0/1000).toFixed(2))}B in the year-ago TTM window).</p>\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;margin-top:10px;\">Drivers of the inflection map cleanly to public commentary: (1) <strong>ARMv9 royalty rate adoption</strong> accelerated through 2024-2025 as flagship Android handsets (Snapdragon 8 Gen 4, Dimensity 9400) shipped on v9 IP; (2) <strong>Compute Subsystems (CSS)</strong> — bundled IP for mobile and infrastructure — went from new product in FY25-Q1 to \"every major smartphone launch\" by FY26-Q2 (per Rene Haas commentary); (3) <strong>AGI CPU</strong> direct-to-hyperscaler deals (AWS Graviton 5, Google Axion 2nd-gen, Microsoft Cobalt) contributed material royalty inflection from FY26-Q3 onward.</p>\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;margin-top:10px;\">FactSet consensus has FY1 (FY26 ending 3/31/26) revenue at $${(target.sales_FY1_M||0)/1000}B and FY2 (FY27 ending 3/31/27) at $${(target.sales_FY2_M||0)/1000}B — <strong>+32.6% growth FY1→FY2</strong>, the highest in the semiconductor cohort.</p>\n    `, { id:'arm-fa-trajectory' }) : ''}\n\n    ${typeof reportSection === 'function' ? reportSection('Charts & visualizations', `\n      <h4 style=\"margin:8px 0 4px 0;font-size:12.5px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;\">Revenue & growth</h4><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"Revenue trend\"/><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"YoY growth\"/><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"QoQ growth\"/><h4 style=\"margin:14px 0 4px 0;font-size:12.5px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;\">Margins & EPS</h4><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"Margin trend\"/><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"EPS trend\"/><h4 style=\"margin:14px 0 4px 0;font-size:12.5px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;\">Cash flow & capital</h4><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"FCF margin\"/><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"Capex\"/><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"R&D intensity\"/><h4 style=\"margin:14px 0 4px 0;font-size:12.5px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;\">Management credibility</h4><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid var(--border);margin:8px 0;\" alt=\"Guidance vs actual\"/>\n    `, { id:'arm-fa-charts' }) : ''}\n\n    ${typeof reportSection === 'function' ? reportSection('Institutional interpretation — AI-infrastructure operating leverage', `\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;\">The fundamental story is operating leverage on a fixed cost base. ARM's R&D spend is roughly stable at <strong>~$2.5B per year</strong> (40-44% of revenue), while revenue is compounding at +32% over FY1→FY2 per consensus. This is the structural source of the 12pp EBIT margin expansion implied between FY1 (43.9%) and FY2 (45.1%).</p>\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;margin-top:10px;\">Three concurrent drivers, in order of revenue impact: <strong>(1) v9 royalty rate</strong> — industry checks suggest ARMv9 royalty rates are 30-100% above v8 depending on segment, with attach rate now > 25% of total royalty units; <strong>(2) Compute Subsystems (CSS)</strong> — bundled IP that captures 2-3x royalty per chip vs base instruction set; <strong>(3) AGI CPU</strong> — direct sales to hyperscalers (AWS Graviton, Google Axion, Microsoft Cobalt) for AI inference, which carries 5-10x royalty per shipped silicon.</p>\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;margin-top:10px;\">The fundamentals justify a premium multiple in absolute terms. The question is <strong>how much</strong> — 105× P/E FY1 implies revenue growth needs to compound at +30%+ for 3-4 more years to \"grow into\" the valuation. The risk: any sub-10% revenue decel or material EPS miss could trigger material multiple compression (e.g., −30% to peer median 49× P/E would clip $${(target.mkt_cap_B*0.30).toFixed(0)}B of market cap).</p>\n      <p style=\"font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;margin-top:10px;\"><strong>Verdict:</strong> Fundamentals are unambiguously strong — best-in-class management credibility (5B/3H/0M over 8 quarters), structural margin expansion, AI tailwinds with multi-year runway. The trade-off is valuation, not execution.</p>\n    `, { id:'arm-fa-interp', style:'background:rgba(99,102,241,.04);border-left:3px solid var(--accent);' }) : ''}\n  `;\n}\n\n\nfunction armTechnicalPage() {\n  const ta = globalThis.ARM_TA_LEVELS || {};\n  const ti = {\"as_of\": \"2026-05-11\", \"close\": 212.65, \"sma20\": 198.1275, \"sma50\": 160.2911, \"sma100\": 138.08365, \"sma200\": 142.967725, \"atr14\": 18.49108571428571, \"atr_pct\": 8.695549360115546, \"adx14\": 55.42918095824249, \"plus_di\": 47.885177198690876, \"minus_di\": 23.91542272502862, \"high_52w\": 239.5, \"low_52w\": 100.02};\n  return `\n    ${typeof reportHero === 'function' ? reportHero({\n      kicker:'TECHNICAL — Daily bars · SMA · RSI · MACD · ATR · ADX',\n      title:'ARM — Technical Analysis',\n      subtitle:'Spot $${ti.close.toFixed(2)} (as of ${ti.as_of}) · Golden alignment across SMA20/50/100/200 · ADX(14) ${ti.adx14.toFixed(0)} indicates strong trend · ATR(14) ${ti.atr_pct.toFixed(1)}% of price (elevated). 52-week high $${ti.high_52w.toFixed(0)} / low $${ti.low_52w.toFixed(0)}.',\n"
  }
